articles.title: Eurozone January PPI +0.7% vs +0.2% m/m expected
articles.snippet: Prior -0.3%That's a notable bump to start the year and even if you exclude energy prices (+1.3%) from the equation, euro area producer prices were still up 0.6% in January. The breakdown shows increases across the board in the likes of intermediate goods (+1.0%), capital goods (+0.6%), and durable consumer goods (+0.8%). That is all only marginally offset by a decline in prices for non-durable consumer goods (-0.2%) on the month.But when compared to the same month last year, euro area producer p

articles.title: Eurozone February final services PMI 51.9 vs 51.8 prelim
articles.snippet: Prior 51.6Composite PMI 51.9 vs 51.9 prelimPrior 51.3The euro area economy is seen holding up well to start the year amid improved demand conditions. In particular, Germany looks to be one to help shoulder the economic burden after having been the main drag for a long time. The only troubling spot is perhaps that there is a notable rise in cost pressures. Besides that, output charges also remain elevated even if it has eased marginally from January with the rate being the second-steepest in a ye

articles.title: What are the main events for today?
articles.snippet: EUROPEAN SESSIONIn the European session, the main highlight is the Swiss CPI report. The CPI Y/Y is expected at 0.0% vs 0.1% prior. The data isn't going to change anything for the SNB as policymakers have repeatedly stated that the bar for negative interest rates remains very high and they won't move even if they get a couple of deflationary readings (which is what they expect in the coming months). As a reminder, the SNB threatened to intervene on Monday triggering a strong selloff in the Swiss

articles.title: investingLive Americas FX news wrap 3 Mar: Mideast conflict dominate.USD/Gold/Stocks fall.
articles.snippet: S&P and NASDAQ index close down near 1%Trump: DFC to provide political risk insurance and guarantees to all Maritime TradePres. Trump speaking from the White House: Felt strongly Iran was going to attack firstFeds Kashkari: Iran war impact could have monetary policy impactECB's Kazaks: Current rates are appropriate based on what I am seeingFeds Williams: Long-term inflation expectations have remained remarkably stableMore from Feds Williams: AI will change productivity growth, demand for laborFe

articles.title: EUR/JPY pressured by safe-haven flows into Yen amid Middle East war
articles.snippet: EUR/JPY trades around 183.00 on Tuesday at the time of writing, down 0.10% on the day, as the Japanese Yen (JPY) benefits from renewed safe-haven demand amid rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

articles.title: EUR/USD remains subdued near 1.1600 following HCOB PMI data
articles.snippet: EUR/USD extends its losses for the third successive session, trading around 1.1600 during the European hours on Wednesday. The pair holds losses following the release of February’s HCOB Purchasing Managers’ Index (PMI) data from Germany and the Eurozone.

articles.title: Eurozone: Energy shock risks keep ECB on guard – Rabobank
articles.snippet: Rabobank's Senior Macro Strategist Bas van Geffen notes that markets are treating the Middle East conflict as an inflation risk for the Eurozone. He highlights that EUR money markets now partly price an ECB rate hike this year.

articles.title: AUD/USD Price Forecast: Remains below 0.7050, nine-day EMA as bullish bias stabilizes
articles.snippet: AUD/USD pares its daily losses but remains in the negative territory for the second successive day, trading around 0.7030 during the early European hours on Wednesday.

articles.title: Euro area: Inflation supports steady ECB stance – Danske Bank
articles.snippet: Danske Research Team notes that Euro area data show inflation slightly below target but higher than expected, reinforcing expectations that the ECB will keep its policy rate unchanged at 2.00%.

articles.title: EUR/GBP gains ground above 0.8700 on hotter Eurozone inflation data
articles.snippet: The EUR/GBP cross holds positive ground near 0.8710 during the early European session on Wednesday. The Euro (EUR) edges higher against the Pound Sterling (GBP) following hotter-than-expected Eurozone inflation data.

articles.title: EUR/USD: Conflict risks keep euro pressured – Commerzbank
articles.snippet: Commerzbank’s Antje Praefcke argues that geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are increasingly negative for the Euro compared with the Dollar, given Europe’s dependence on energy imports and already sluggish growth.

articles.title: USD/CAD gathers strength to near 1.3700 as safe-haven demand persists
articles.snippet: USD/CAD edges higher to near 1.3695 during the early European trading hours, bolstered by broader US Dollar (USD) bid. The pair has been trending lower since the January highs close to 1.3930, carving out a series of lower highs and lower lows.

articles.title: Pound Sterling weakens to near 1.3300 as geopolitical risks bolster US Dollar
articles.snippet: The GBP/USD pair attracts some sellers to around 1.3310 during the early European session on Wednesday. Escalating conflict in the Middle East triggers a "flight to safety," supporting the US Dollar (USD) against the Pound Sterling (GBP).

articles.title: USD/CHF weakens to near 0.7800 as Middle East tensions lift Swiss Franc
articles.snippet: The USD/CHF pair loses traction to around 0.7805 during the early European session on Wednesday. The Swiss Franc (CHF) gathers strength against the Greenback on safe-haven flows stemming from Middle East tensions.

articles.title: AI may be creating instead of destroying jobs for now, ECB blog argues
Euro and yen slide as Middle East conflict escalates
articles.snippet: March 2 : The euro and yen fell on Monday as concerns about higher oil prices dented the currencies of countries most exposed to energy shortages, while the dollar also benefited from a safe-haven bid on concerns about conflict in the Middle East widening.The…

[0.50] EUR/JPY pressured by safe-haven flows into Yen amid Middle East war
EUR/JPY trades around 183.00 on Tuesday at the time of writing, down 0.10% on the day, as the Japanese Yen (JPY) benefits from renewed safe-haven demand amid rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

[1.00] EUR/USD remains subdued near 1.1600 following HCOB PMI data
EUR/USD extends its losses for the third successive session, trading around 1.1600 during the European hours on Wednesday. The pair holds losses following the release of February’s HCOB Purchasing Managers’ Index (PMI) data from Germany and the Eurozone.

[1.00] Eurozone: Energy shock risks keep ECB on guard – Rabobank
Rabobank's Senior Macro Strategist Bas van Geffen notes that markets are treating the Middle East conflict as an inflation risk for the Eurozone. He highlights that EUR money markets now partly price an ECB rate hike this year.

[0.67] AUD/USD Price Forecast: Remains below 0.7050, nine-day EMA as bullish bias stabilizes
AUD/USD pares its daily losses but remains in the negative territory for the second successive day, trading around 0.7030 during the early European hours on Wednesday.

[1.00] Euro area: Inflation supports steady ECB stance – Danske Bank
Danske Research Team notes that Euro area data show inflation slightly below target but higher than expected, reinforcing expectations that the ECB will keep its policy rate unchanged at 2.00%.

[1.00] EUR/GBP gains ground above 0.8700 on hotter Eurozone inflation data
The EUR/GBP cross holds positive ground near 0.8710 during the early European session on Wednesday. The Euro (EUR) edges higher against the Pound Sterling (GBP) following hotter-than-expected Eurozone inflation data.

[0.67] EUR/USD: Conflict risks keep euro pressured – Commerzbank
Commerzbank’s Antje Praefcke argues that geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are increasingly negative for the Euro compared with the Dollar, given Europe’s dependence on energy imports and already sluggish growth.

[0.67] USD/CAD gathers strength to near 1.3700 as safe-haven demand persists
USD/CAD edges higher to near 1.3695 during the early European trading hours, bolstered by broader US Dollar (USD) bid. The pair has been trending lower since the January highs close to 1.3930, carving out a series of lower highs and lower lows.

[0.67] Pound Sterling weakens to near 1.3300 as geopolitical risks bolster US Dollar 
The GBP/USD pair attracts some sellers to around 1.3310 during the early European session on Wednesday. Escalating conflict in the Middle East triggers a "flight to safety," supporting the US Dollar (USD) against the Pound Sterling (GBP).

[0.67] USD/CHF weakens to near 0.7800 as Middle East tensions lift Swiss Franc 
The USD/CHF pair loses traction to around 0.7805 during the early European session on Wednesday. The Swiss Franc (CHF) gathers strength against the Greenback on safe-haven flows stemming from Middle East tensions.
